uses: Aria intermedia, also known as the Sargent Cherry or the Sargent's Cherry, is not a well-documented species for its medicinal uses. However, its close relatives in the Prunus genus have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The bark, leaves, and fruits of some Prunus species have been used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. Some Prunus species are also used to make medicinal teas and infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Further research is needed to confirm the specific medicinal uses of Aria intermedia.
